ENGAA-2021-M1-001: Simplify fully $5xy^2 \times (5x^2y)^{-3} \times 5x^2y$, where $x$ and $y$ are positive.
ENGAA-2021-PHY-001: Air is trapped in a cylinder by a piston. The density of the trapped air is $\rho$. The piston is moved so that the pressure of the trapped air increases by 20%. The temperature of the trapped air does not change. What is the new density of the trapped air? (Assume that air is an ideal gas.)
ENGAA-2021-M1-002: Which of the following is a rearrangement of $p = 3 + \dfrac{4}{qr}$ so that $q$ is the subject?
ENGAA-2021-PHY-002: A non-ideal transformer has 100 turns on the primary coil and 25 turns on the secondary coil. It is provided with 3.0 kW of electrical power at a current of 12.5 A. The voltage output is the same as for an ideal transformer, but the current in the output coil is 40 A. What is the efficiency of the transformer?
ENGAA-2021-M1-003: Two solid cylinders, P and Q, are shown, where $x>y$. Cylinder P has diameter $x$ and height $y$. Cylinder Q has diameter $y$ and height $x$. What is the positive difference between the total surface areas of P and Q?
ENGAA-2021-PHY-003: A light spring has an uncompressed length of 0.10 m. When an object of mass 0.5 kg rests in equilibrium on top of the spring, the length of the spring reduces to 0.08 m. What is the energy stored in the spring due to the compression? (gravitational field strength = 10 N kg$^{-1}$; the spring obeys Hooke's law)
ENGAA-2021-M1-004: The price of item P is reduced by 10%. The next day, the new price is increased by 10%. The price of item Q is increased by 10%. The next day, the new price is reduced by 10%. How does the final price of each item compare to the original price of that item?
ENGAA-2021-PHY-004: A set of decorative lights consists of 20 lamps connected in series to a dc supply of constant voltage. The total power transferred by all the lamps is P. The set is designed so that if one of the lamps fails, that lamp becomes short-circuited (zero resistance); the remaining lamps stay lit. If this happens, with the set connected to the same supply, what is the new total power transferred by the remaining 19 lamps? (Assume the resistance of each functioning lamp remains constant.)
ENGAA-2021-M1-005: [DIAGRAM: Triangle SQT is right-angled at Q, with point R on SQ such that SR:RQ = 1:3. Triangle QRP is right-angled at Q. PR = ST = 8 cm, QT = 4 cm.] What is the length of PQ, in cm?
ENGAA-2021-PHY-005: [DIAGRAM: graph of momentum (×10^6 kg m s^-1) vs time (s) for an accelerating train, a straight line rising from the origin to about 12×10^6 kg m s^-1 at t = 4 s] A train accelerates from rest along a straight, horizontal track. The force exerted by its motors is constant and there is a constant friction force of $1.8\times10^7$ N. The graph shows how the momentum of the train changes with time. What is the force exerted on the train by its motors?
ENGAA-2021-M1-006: The curve with equation $y=x^2-4x+5$ meets the straight line with equation $y=2x+c$ at two points, which have x-coordinates $p$ and $q$, where $q>p$. Given that $q-p=8$, what is the value of the constant $c$?
ENGAA-2021-PHY-006: A ship travels into a wave that is travelling in the opposite direction to the ship. The ship has a horizontal speed of 8.0 m s$^{-1}$. The speed of the wave is 3.0 m s$^{-1}$. The front of the ship rises and falls with a time period of 8.0 s. What is the wavelength of the wave?
ENGAA-2021-M2-001: Given that $y=\dfrac{\sin(-160°)}{\cos60°}$, what is the value of $y^3$?
ENGAA-2021-PHY-007: [DIAGRAM: circuit with a 6.0 V battery connected to an 8.0 Ω resistor in series with a filament lamp and an ammeter] The reading on the ammeter is 0.25 A. Which graph is a possible V-I graph for the filament lamp? (Graphs A-F show V (up to 2.0, 4.0, or 6.0 V) plotted against I (0 to 0.25 A), with curves of varying shape.)
ENGAA-2021-M1-007: Charlie has a bowl containing red sweets and green sweets only. The sweets are identical in all respects except colour. There are nine sweets in total in the bowl. Charlie eats two sweets from the bowl at random. The probability of Charlie not eating any green sweets is $\frac{5}{12}$. What is the probability that Charlie eats two green sweets?
ENGAA-2021-PHY-008: A radioactive nuclide X decays in a single stage to a stable nuclide R. A radioactive nuclide Y decays in a single stage to a stable nuclide S. When a rock formed it contained equal numbers of atoms of all four nuclides X, Y, R and S. The half-life of X is $T$ years and the half-life of Y is $2T$ years. What is the value of $\dfrac{\text{number of atoms of S}}{\text{number of atoms of R}}$ at a time $4T$ years after the rock formed? (Assume no other processes add or remove X, Y, R or S from the rock.)
ENGAA-2021-M1-008: The greatest diagonal distance between two vertices of a cuboid is $\sqrt{77}$ cm. A similar cuboid has all its lengths exactly half the lengths of the original cuboid. The sides of this smaller cuboid are 2 cm, 3 cm and $x$ cm. What is the value of $x$, in cm?
ENGAA-2021-PHY-009: A beaker containing 180 g of water at 25 °C has a 20 g ice cube at 0 °C added to it. No heat is transferred between the water and the surroundings (including the beaker). What is the final temperature of all the water in the beaker after all the ice has melted? (Specific heat capacity of water = 4 J g$^{-1}$ °C$^{-1}$; specific latent heat of fusion of water = 300 J g$^{-1}$.)
ENGAA-2021-M1-009: A car journey is $m$ miles long. One kilometre is equivalent to $x$ miles. The car uses one litre of fuel to travel a distance of $f$ kilometres. Fuel for the car costs $p$ pence per litre. Which of the following expressions gives the cost of fuel for this journey, in pounds? (There are 100 pence in one pound.)
ENGAA-2021-PHY-010: A pulse of ultrasound travels from one end of a solid uniform rod of length $L$, starting at time $t=0$. The pulse is partially reflected by a crack in the rod and partially by the far end of the rod. These two reflected pulses travel back along the rod, arriving at the end from which they started at times $t_1$ and $t_2$, where $t_2>t_1$. What is the distance between the crack and the far end of the rod?
ENGAA-2021-M2-002: Given that $y=\left(2\sqrt{x}-\dfrac{1}{2\sqrt{x}}\right)^2$, find the value of $\dfrac{dy}{dx}$ when $x=\dfrac12$.
ENGAA-2021-PHY-011: Object P of mass 2.4 kg is on a smooth plane inclined at 60° to the horizontal. A constant force of magnitude $2F$ parallel to the plane is applied to P, so P moves directly up the plane with constant velocity. Object Q of mass 0.75 kg is on a smooth, horizontal plane. A constant force of magnitude $F$ parallel to the plane is applied to Q, so Q moves with constant acceleration. What is the acceleration of Q? (gravitational field strength = 10 N kg$^{-1}$)
ENGAA-2021-M2-003: A particular arithmetic series has first term $a$ and common difference $d$. The sum of the first $k$ terms of this series is denoted $S_k$. Which of the following is a simplification of $S_{n+1}-S_{n-1}$?
ENGAA-2021-PHY-012: [DIAGRAM: waveform of a sound wave travelling left to right in air, with a distance of 33 cm labelled spanning several cycles, and a point P marked on the wave] The speed of sound in air is 330 m s$^{-1}$. What is the frequency of the sound and in which direction has the air at P been displaced from its mean position?
ENGAA-2021-M2-004: Find how many distinct real solutions there are to the equation $(x^2+4x+3)^2=1$.
ENGAA-2021-PHY-013: [DIAGRAM: circuit with a 30 V dc supply, an 8.0 Ω resistor in series with a parallel combination of a 4.0 Ω heater and a resistor R connected between terminals X and Y] The power transferred in the 4.0 Ω heater is 9.0 W. What is the resistance of R?
ENGAA-2021-M2-005: The line $x=1$ divides the circle $x^2+y^2=4$ into two segments. What is the area of the smaller segment?
ENGAA-2021-PHY-014: [DIAGRAM: uniform plank of length 5.0 m resting horizontally, with a pivot 1.0 m from one end, and a cable at 60° to the horizontal supporting the other end, tension 75 N] What is the weight of the plank?
ENGAA-2021-M2-006: What is the mean of $\log_{10}27$, $\log_{10}64$, and $\log_{10}216$?
ENGAA-2021-PHY-015: [DIAGRAM: straight horizontal road showing four successive oil droplets dripped from an accelerating lorry at constant time interval $t$; the distance between the first two droplets is $x$ and between the final two is $y$] A lorry accelerates along a straight, horizontal road with uniform acceleration. Oil droplets fall from the lorry onto the road at a constant time interval $t$. Which expression gives the acceleration of the lorry?
ENGAA-2021-M2-007: Which of the following is the largest in value? (All angles are in radians.)
ENGAA-2021-PHY-016: A light, metal wire of length 2.5 m and cross-sectional area $1.8\times10^{-6}$ m$^2$ is suspended vertically. A mass of 7.2 kg is attached to the lower end of the wire. The wire extends by 0.50 mm. What is the Young modulus of the metal and how much energy is stored in the extended wire? (gravitational field strength = 10 N kg$^{-1}$; assume Hooke's law holds and cross-sectional area change is negligible)
ENGAA-2021-M2-008: A geometric progression has first term $u_1=a$ and common ratio $r$. The sum to infinity of the geometric progression is $\frac{8}{5}$. The sum to infinity of the even-numbered terms ($u_2+u_4+u_6+\ldots$) is $\frac{3}{5}$. What is the value of $a+r$?
ENGAA-2021-PHY-017: A child of mass 30 kg is on a sledge of mass 10 kg which is moving down a smooth slope at an instantaneous speed of 4.0 m s$^{-1}$. At this instant, the child jumps backwards off the sledge and lands stationary on the slope. What is the speed of the sledge immediately after the child jumps off?
ENGAA-2021-M2-009: At how many distinct points do the following two curves meet? $y=(x-4)(x^2-2x-8)$ and $y=-x^2+8x-16$
ENGAA-2021-PHY-018: A piece of electrically conducting putty is formed into the shape of a uniform cylinder. The resistance between the ends of the cylinder is $R$. The same piece of putty is now formed into a new uniform cylinder with half the diameter of the first cylinder. What is the resistance between the ends of the new cylinder?
ENGAA-2021-PHY-019: A car accelerates from rest in a straight line. During the first 10 s, its acceleration, $a$, in m s$^{-2}$ is given by $a=4.0-0.36t$, where $t$ is the time in seconds. What is its displacement from its original position after 10 s?
ENGAA-2021-M2-011: [DIAGRAM: PQRS is a rectangle with P and Q on the x-axis, Q and R on the line x = 15, and S on the curve y = √x] What is the maximum possible area of the rectangle?
ENGAA-2021-PHY-020: Two trolleys are free to move on a smooth one-dimensional track. A light spring is compressed between the two stationary trolleys; the trolleys are released and separate. The trolleys have masses $m$ and $4m$, and the work done by the spring as it expands is $W$. Assume no work is done against friction. What is the difference in kinetic energy between the two trolleys when the spring has fully expanded?
